assertions.title: Recovery persistence across sessions

steps.expected: Landing page loads with hero and CTA

steps.action: Click Get Started to sign up
Fill email and password, submit signup
Navigate to /plan/new to create a plan
steps.expected: New plan page with chat interface loads
steps.observed: Plan creation page loaded with chat input

steps.action: Send message to generate plan: 'I'm at Santa Monica College transferring to UCLA for Computer Science'
steps.expected: AI generates a semester plan with courses
steps.observed: OpenRouter rate limit error: 'Rate limited, try again in a moment'

steps.action: Attempt to mark a course as failed and access recovery options
steps.expected: Course status menu appears with options: completed, in-progress, cancelled, waitlisted, failed
steps.observed: Course status tracking UI not implemented - course cards have no click handlers or status menus

steps.expected: After marking failed, AI recovery conversation triggers automatically
steps.observed: AI recovery flow not implemented in the UI

evidence.network: POST to OpenRouter API returned rate limit error

assertions.issues: This assertion is BLOCKED because: (1) The course status tracking feature is NOT implemented in the UI - course cards have no click handlers for marking status, (2) The AI recovery flow is NOT implemented, (3) OpenRouter rate limit prevents plan generation. According to the task notes, these features belong to the 'failure-recovery' milestone, not 'plan-generation'. The database schema exists (plan_courses table with status field, failure_events table) but the UI implementation is missing.

frictions.description: OpenRouter API rate limit (20 req/min, 50 req/day) prevents consistent testing of AI-dependent features
The app was running on port 3003 instead of the expected port 3000, which required adjusting the test URL

blockers.description: Course status tracking and AI recovery features are not implemented in the UI. The database schema supports these features (plan_courses.status, plan_courses.alternative_for, failure_events table) but there are no UI components for: (1) clicking a course to show status options, (2) marking a course as failed/cancelled/waitlisted, (3) triggering AI recovery flow, (4) accepting alternative courses. These features are documented in the validation contract under 'Area: Failure Recovery' (VAL-RECOVER-001 through VAL-RECOVER-012) but the UI code (CourseCard, SemesterPlan, PlanDetailClient) does not implement them.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Reviewed codebase: components/course-card.tsx has no click handlers or status UI; components/semester-plan.tsx has no status integration; app/(protected)/plan/[id]/plan-detail-client.tsx has no status management or recovery flow. Per task notes, these features belong to 'failure-recovery' milestone.

blockers.description: OpenRouter API rate limit hit - unable to generate plan for testing. Error message: 'Rate limited, try again in a moment'
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Attempted plan generation once, received rate limit error immediately. Cannot proceed with testing recovery features without a generated plan.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 0 passed, 0 failed, 1 blocked. VAL-CROSS-006 is blocked because the required features (course status tracking and AI recovery) are not implemented in the current codebase. These features are planned for the 'failure-recovery' milestone. Additionally, OpenRouter rate limit prevents plan generation, which is a prerequisite for testing recovery.
